note: Funding stack assumed for an infrastructure-first entry. All amounts and eligibility must be verified against current program guidelines before commitment.

programs.name: Zero Emission Vehicle Infrastructure Program (ZEVIP)
programs.typicalShare: Up to 50% of eligible project costs
programs.notes: Primary federal lever for charging deployment. Application windows open periodically.

programs.name: Canada Infrastructure Bank — ZEV Charging Initiative
programs.typicalShare: Up to 80% of eligible capex (project-finance style)
programs.notes: Suited for portfolios of sites with predictable utilization.

programs.name: Smart Renewables and Electrification Pathways (SREPs)
programs.notes: Useful when storage is paired with charging to defer interconnect upgrades.

programs.name: Strategic Innovation Fund (SIF)
programs.agency: Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada
programs.notes: Relevant only at the local-assembly / JV phase, not at validation phase.

programs.name: Ontario Save On Energy / EV ChargeON
programs.typicalShare: Up to 75% of eligible costs at qualifying sites
fitFor: Public charging in smaller communities and along highway corridors
programs.notes: Stackable with ZEVIP in many cases; verify per intake.

programs.name: Roulez vert / Hydro-Québec programs
programs.notes: QC has the deepest EV adoption; partnership with Hydro-Québec is strategically critical.

programs.name: CleanBC Go Electric
fitFor: Public, workplace, multi-unit, and fleet charging
programs.notes: BC Hydro is an active partner for managed charging programs.
